questions.question: Derive the Boolean expressions for Sum (S) and Carry (C) outputs of a Half Adder. Also write the truth table for a Half Adder showing all possible input combinations.
keyPoints: Sum (S) = A XOR B = A'B + AB'
Half Adder can only add 2 single-bit numbers, cannot handle carry input

questions.question: Design a 1-bit Full Adder using two Half Adders and one OR gate. Draw the circuit diagram and verify that the output matches the Full Adder truth table.
keyPoints: First Half Adder adds A and B, produces Sum1 and Carry1
Second Half Adder adds Sum1 and Cin, produces Sum (S) and Carry2
Final Carry (Cout) = Carry1 OR Carry2 = AB + ACin + BCin
Circuit implements: S = A ⊕ B ⊕ Cin, Cout = AB + (A ⊕ B)Cin

questions.question: (a) Write the complete truth table for a 1-bit Full Adder with inputs A, B, and Cin. (b) Derive the simplified Boolean expressions for Sum (S) and Carry-out (Cout) using Boolean algebra. (c) Implement the Full Adder using appropriate logic gates and explain the propagation delay for each output path.
keyPoints: Truth table has 8 combinations: S = A ⊕ B ⊕ Cin, Cout = AB + ACin + BCin
Simplified expressions: S = A'B'Cin + A'BCin' + AB'Cin' + ABCin, Cout = AB + ACin + BCin
Propagation delay: Cout path (2 gate delays via XOR+AND+OR), Sum path (2 gate delays via XOR)
In cascade adders (e.g., ripple carry), each Cout feeds next stage Cin, causing cumulative delay O(n)

questions.question: Explain the limitation of a Half Adder in arithmetic operations. How does a Full Adder overcome this limitation? Design a 4-bit parallel adder using four Full Adders and discuss the critical path delay, including the effect of ripple carry propagation on maximum operating frequency. Also mention one method to reduce this delay.
keyPoints: Half Adder cannot add multi-bit numbers as it lacks carry input (Cin); only handles 2 single-bit numbers
Full Adder includes Cin and Cout, enabling cascade connection for multi-bit addition
4-bit ripple carry adder: worst-case delay = 8 gate delays (Cin propagates through all 4 stages)
Delay reduction techniques: carry look-ahead adder, carry select adder, pipelining; reduces O(n) to O(log n)
